items.title: Elderly Kedah school bus driver turns to funeral transport work after losing students, vehicle seized by JPJ
items.summary: BALING, May 17 — A 73-year-old school bus driver in Kedah was forced to turn his vehicle into a paid passenger transport service after struggling to find students to ferry over the past two years, leading to the bus being seized by the Road Transport Department (JPJ) for allegedly misusing its permit. Kedah JPJ director Stien Van Lutam said investigations found that the bus had not been transporting schoolchildren for the past two years due to a lack of demand. Although the vehicle had valid insurance coverage and road tax, Van Lutam said it had breached the conditions of its operator’s licence by carrying passengers other than school students.

items.title: Govt focused on BUDI rollout, not T20 reclassification, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the cabinet has not discussed any new classification involving the T20 income group. He said the government's current focus is on the nationwide implementation of the BUDI fuel subsidy mechanism rather than introducing new income category classifications. "As explained by Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil last week, the cabinet did not discuss any T-category classifications," he said.

items.title: Amir Hamzah to seek audience with Pahang Sultan this week over federal allocation concerns
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, May 17 — Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan will seek an audience with the Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah in the near future to clarify issues regarding the Federal Government’s allocation to the state of Pahang. Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan said the Federal Government’s commitment is clear in ensuring that every allocation to the state is made based on needs and the Federal Constitution. On May 8, the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, while officiating the Opening Ceremony of the First Meeting of the Fifth Term of the 15th Pahang State Legislative Assembly, questioned the Federal Government’s allocations to the state.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues nationwide thunderstorm, heavy rain warning until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds expected to hit nearly the entire country until 7pm today. In Kedah, the affected areas include Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, and Padang Terap, while in Perak, the areas involved are Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim. For Pahang, the warning covers Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Kuantan, Pekan, and Rompin, while in Selangor, the affected areas are Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat.

items.title: Matrix Concepts unveils lifestyle hub in Kluang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has launched X Park Bandar Seri Impian, a recreational and lifestyle destination in Kluang, Johor. Matrix Concepts said the launch marks the second X Park township collaboration after Bandar Sri Sendayan, positioning Bandar Seri Impian as another hub for recreational activities. Matrix Concepts chairman Datuk Mohamad Haslah Mohamad Amin said X Park Bandar Seri Impian is more than a recreational facility; it serves as a community hub that brings people together through shared activities and experiences.

items.title: Anwar warns allies: You betray us, we'll fight back
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today issued one of his strongest warnings yet amid growing tensions within the unity government, declaring that PH would retaliate if any party attempted to betray or undermine the coalition. "We have never incited or plotted betrayal against anyone," Anwar said during his keynote address at the 2026 PH Convention held at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre today. he said.

items.title: Loke: 80pct of UEC students sat for SPM
items.summary: Loke, who is the Democratic Action Party (DAP) secretary-general, said the facts clearly showed that more than 80 per cent of UEC students also sat for the SPM examination, including 9,580 out of 10,944 students last year. He said this had convinced the Cabinet that students from the UEC stream would continue to take the national examination as the foundation of their education. "UEC students also sit for the SPM.

items.title: Anwar signals early polls if unity government pressures persist
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim warned political partners in national unity government today that he is prepared to call for an early general election if their political "intimidation" and threats persist. Speaking at the 2026 Pakatan Harapan Convention here, Anwar, who is the PH chairman, directly referenced the ongoing state-level tensions, escalating the situation to a national scale. “If it is true that they feel it is time for the election to take place in Johor, we will continue in Negeri Sembilan, and perhaps the people can decide for the whole of Malaysia,” he said in his keynote address.

items.title: Iran chief negotiator Ghalibaf appointed to oversee ties with China
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who recently emerged as a chief negotiator in talks with the United States, has been appointed to oversee relations with China, Iranian media reported on Sunday. "Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has recently been appointed as a special representative of the Islamic republic of Iran for China affairs," Tasnim news agency reported, citing "informed sources", with other media carrying similar reports. Fars news agency said late security chief Ali Larijani, who was killed in US-Israeli strikes on March 17, held a similar position.

items.title: Zambry: Higher education access must align with national policy, unity goals, stresses BM and History requirements for UEC holders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — The government is adopting a balanced, inclusive and responsible approach in its efforts to expand access to national higher education as part of building a common meeting point for Malaysia’s diverse society. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the commitment must nevertheless remain aligned with efforts to uphold the foundations of the National Education Policy and the National Education Philosophy. “It also involves nation-building, strengthening unity, empowering Bahasa Melayu as the national language, as well as understanding Malaysia’s history and nationhood.

items.title: ‘Silk Road’ Scholarship offers full sponsorship for Malaysian undergraduates, postgraduates in China
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 17 — Malaysian students can now apply for the Chinese Government’s fully funded “Silk Road” Scholarship Programme for the 2026 intake, offered by Kibing Group in partnership with Changsha University of Science and Technology (CSUST) and supported by Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). The scholarship will fully sponsor five undergraduate students and five to 10 master’s students in fields related to new energy and engineering. The undergraduate programme in New Energy Science and Engineering will be taught in Mandarin, while the master’s programme in Power Engineering and Engineering Thermophysics is offered in Mandarin or English.

items.title: Amir Hamzah: IBS modular method speeds up school construction
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: The use of the Industrialised Building System (IBS) modular method in school construction can reduce costs by 20 to 30 per cent and enable faster project completion. He said the initiative aims to ensure children, particularly in rural areas, are not left behind due to inadequate educational facilities. Amir Hamzah said the IBS modular system offers advantages, including faster completion time and lower costs.

items.title: Talk to your own BN minister: Loke tells Wee Ka Siong where to bring UEC grouses
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has told MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong to direct his complaints regarding the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) to his own coalition partner, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. Speaking at the 2026 Pakatan Harapan Convention today, Loke pointed out that since Zambry is from Barisan Nasional (BN) and MCA is a component party, any dissatisfaction should be resolved internally within their coalition. So, don’t blame Anthony Loke and DAP.

items.title: Higher education must align with National Education Policy, unity goals – Zambry
items.summary: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said this commitment must remain aligned with efforts to uphold the foundations of the National Education Policy and National Education Philosophy. "It also involves nation-building, strengthening unity, empowering Bahasa Melayu as the national language, and understanding Malaysia's history and nationhood. "For this reason, the government cannot alter the foundations of the national education system simply to satisfy the demands of any particular educational stream," Zambry said.

items.title: Gagasan Rakyat amends constitution, aligns divisions with 26 parliamentary seats, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 17 — Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah (Gagasan Rakyat) is fully prepared to face the 16th General Election (GE16) following a constitutional amendment to restructure its divisions according to the 26 parliamentary constituencies in Sabah and Labuan. Its president Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor said the move would strengthen the party’s grassroots machinery and streamline preparations ahead of the parliamentary polls. “We are ready to face the coming election, organise our party strategy and strengthen the machinery based on parliamentary constituencies.

items.title: 'Taiwan independence' means we don't belong to Beijing, president says
items.summary: TAIPEI: The expression "Taiwan independence" means the island neither belongs to nor is subordinate to Beijing and that only the Taiwanese people can decide their future, President Lai Ching-te said on Sunday. "It refers to the Republic of China and the People's Republic of China not being subordinate to each other," Lai added. "Whether we call it the Republic of China, the Republic of China, Taiwan, or Taiwan — basically, no matter what we are called internationally, it refers to us: the 23 million people of Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu."

items.title: China's market regulator outlines measures to support private economy
items.summary: BEIJING: China's State Administration for Market Regulation has released a 2026 work plan to support the development of the private economy, outlining 34 key tasks aimed at creating a fairer market environment and improving regulatory services, reported Xinhua. The plan calls for equal emphasis on standardised regulation and development promotion, pledging efforts to foster fair competition, innovate regulatory approaches and strengthen services to support the high-quality development of the private sector. Efforts will be made to expand credit-based incentives, strengthen technical trade support and enhance policy measures for individual businesses, according to the plan.

items.title: Now, Loke wants Pakatan to contest all 36 seats in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: Loke declared that PH should contest all 36 state seats in Negeri Sembilan, in direct retaliation to Johor BN’s announcement yesterday that it would contest all 56 seats in the upcoming state election. “We will also contest all 56 state seats in Johor, and I suggest that PH in Negeri Sembilan do the same and contest all 36 state seats,” he said. “If the Johor state assembly is dissolved tomorrow, we will also dissolve the Negeri Sembilan state assembly tomorrow,” he declared.

items.title: Non-state actors are reshaping the Palestinian narrative
items.summary: The renewed attention surrounding humanitarian flotillas comes amid growing international scrutiny over humanitarian access to Gaza, increasing criticism of diplomatic paralysis and widening public frustration over the limited effectiveness of international institutions. According to Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) political analyst, Prof Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian, the Global Sumud Flotilla reflects the continuing geopolitical significance of Palestine within international relations. "The Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) reflects the persistence of the Palestine issue as a moral and political fault line in international relations.

items.title: [UPDATED] Snap election on the table if unity pact breaks down, says Anwar
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today signalled Pakatan Harapan's (PH) readiness for a full-scale electoral showdown, saying the coalition was prepared for a snap general election should ties within the unity government deteriorate further. The prime minister warned that PH would respond "firmly" if political rivals chose confrontation over cooperation. His remarks came amid growing tensions within the unity government following Johor BN's decision to contest all 56 state seats and DAP's indication that it was also prepared for a full contest in Negri Sembilan if needed.

items.title: [UPDATED] Amir Hamzah to meet Sultan of Pahang over federal allocation issues
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan will meet the Sultan of Pahang before the end of next week to clarify issues regarding the federal government's allocation to the state. Amir Hamzah said the federal government remains committed to ensuring state allocations are provided in accordance with budget provisions, while efforts are also being made to increase allocations annually. This follows remarks by the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, who reportedly called for federal government intervention in matters involving the state's revenue, including approval for river sand exports.

items.title: Labuan Corp lodges police report over viral online casino allegation tied to Pulau Papan
items.summary: LABUAN, May 17 — Labuan Corporation (LC) has lodged a police report calling for a thorough investigation into a viral Facebook post that allegedly tarnished its image by linking it to a proposed casino development on Pulau Papan. LC chief executive officer Rithuan Ismail said the report was lodged at the Labuan police station on May 15 following the posting, which had used the photographs of him and LC deputy chairman Simsudin Sidek. Rithuan said Pulau Papan, one of Labuan’s well-known island attractions, has strong potential in sustainable tourism, recreational activities, nature-based products, heritage elements and family-oriented attractions.

items.title: Cabinet greenlights RM10b rapid transit project for JB, Loke confirms
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The federal government has approved the long-awaited rapid transit project aimed at alleviating chronic traffic congestion here, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. “The Cabinet has just approved the RM10 billion project. Loke added that the transit project is crucial to support the upcoming Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, which is scheduled to begin operations in 2027.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: JS‑SEZ proof of Madani govt’s economic strategy success, accounts for 66pc of Johor investments
items.summary: Pakatan Harapan (PH) secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the unique project, resulting from cooperation between the two countries, has successfully attracted major investments, accounting for 66 per cent of Johor’s total investments over the past year. “We are replacing outdated border management systems, expediting procurement programmes, building new systems, and putting in place the most advanced infrastructure. He said this in his speech at the PH Convention 2026 at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.

items.title: Thunderstorms to lash large swathes of Malaysia until late evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for states across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak, cautioning residents to brace for heavy rain, strong winds and lightning until 7.00pm. In Peninsular Malaysia, the affected states include Perlis, several districts in Kedah — namely Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena and Padang Terap — as well as districts in Perak, Pahang, Selangor, Negeri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor. The warning covers densely populated and economically significant areas, including parts of the Klang Valley such as Gombak, Hulu Selangor and Hulu Langat.

items.title: Madani govt resolves nine MA63 matters in three years
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Madani government has achieved the best record in the nation's history by resolving nine matters under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) within just three years of administration. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said the achievement surpassed earlier administrations, which managed to resolve only four matters over a longer period. This involves negotiations among the three key regions that formed Malaysia," Mustapha said.

items.title: Voters judging PH sincerity, warns Adam Adli at party convention
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR Youth chief Adam Adli urged Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders to uphold the coalition's reformist values, warning voters were closely judging their sincerity and resolve. PH's strength had always rested on its governance model centred on integrity, public welfare and accountability, rather than political power or personal enrichment, he said. Adam Adli's remarks come amid growing speculation over a possible snap Johor state election following viral WhatsApp messages alleging a special state assembly sitting on June 24.

items.title: Remand extended for man accused of abusing 5-year-old stepson
items.summary: Acting district police chief Deputy Superintendent Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said the extension allows police to complete investigations under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001. He said the suspect was previously remanded for a week, a period which expired today. "Investigations revealed that the suspect, who has two children, married the victim's mother, who also has two children, three years ago," he said.

items.title: Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi quit PKR, will vacate Pandan and Setiawangsa seats
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 17 — Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said they will vacate the Pandan and Setiawangsa seats tomorrow. The duo said they would notify the Dewan Rakyat Speaker of their withdrawal tomorrow. Rafizi and Nik Nazmi also said they would formally write to the PKR secretary-general on Tuesday to inform the party of their decision.

items.title: [UPDATED] Rafizi, Nik Nazmi to leave PKR, vacate parliamentary seats
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Pandan and Setiawangsa members of arliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today announced that they will vacate their respective seats, effective tomorrow. Nik Nazmi followed suit, confirming that he would also vacate his parliamentary seat. "I hereby announce that I will vacate the Setiawangsa parliamentary seat effective tomorrow," he said.

items.title: Johor BN, Pakatan contesting all seats won’t affect federal cooperation, says Fahmi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The decision by Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) in Johor to each contest all 56 seats in the next state election will not affect cooperation at the federal government level, said PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said the stance expressed by Johor BN and PH was made at the state level and does not impact the existing cooperative relationship. Yesterday, Johor Menteri Besar and state BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the decision to contest all state seats, followed by Johor PH chief Aminolhuda Hassan, who made a similar announcement when speaking at the convention today.

items.title: JS-SEZ demonstrates Madani govt's economic gains in Johor - Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: Pakatan Harapan (PH) secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the unique project, resulting from cooperation between the two countries, has successfully attracted major investments, accounting for 66 per cent of Johor's total investments over the past year. He said to support the growth of the economic region, the government is placing strong emphasis on connectivity and mobility at the country's entry points to ensure it is no longer constrained by outdated management systems. "We are replacing outdated border management systems, expediting procurement programmes, building new systems, and putting in place the most advanced infrastructure.

items.title: Cardiac anaesthesiologist finds new peace in farming
items.summary: Dr Saedah Ali, 59, who began her career as a medical doctor in 1992 and was involved in pioneering cardiac surgery services at HPUSM in 2001, ventured into livestock farming in June 2018, about four years before taking optional retirement in 2022. "There is a form of communication and emotional connection when raising livestock. Besides sheep, she also raises Boer goats and Boer crossbreeds for meat production.

items.title: SAR ends after second drowned sibling found in Kota Belud river
items.summary: KOTA BELUD: Search and rescue (SAR) operations for two siblings who drowned in a river near a campsite in Polumpung Melangkap Baru have ended following the recovery of the second body. District Fire and Rescue Station chief Elge Bakuk said the second day of SAR operations began at 8.50am, with the search split into three sectors. Sector A covered a 3.4km stretch along the river from Polumpung Melangkap to Sondot View Camp, while Sector B spanned 5.2km from SK Narinang to Sondot View Camp.

items.title: Pas lacks other capable leaders, Guan Eng says after Samsuri's appointment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng has questioned Pas' decision to appoint Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as Opposition Leader while he continues serving as Terengganu Menteri Besar, saying the move places him under two heavy responsibilities simultaneously. "The question arises whether Pas has no capable leader apart from Ahmad Samsuri to shoulder these two responsibilities by wearing 'two hats' as Opposition Leader and Terengganu Menteri Besar. "Both positions carry heavy duties and responsibilities.

items.title: Missing teen found drowned in Kadamaian River tragedy near Kota Belud, brother found yesterday
items.summary: KOTA BELUD, May 17 — The second victim of a river incident at Sungai Kadamaian near Melangkap Home Stay Polumpung View Camp Site was found at 12.04pm today. His elder brother, Zul Hakim Abdullah, 23, had earlier been found drowned yesterday. Fire and Rescue personnel said search and rescue operations began after an emergency call yesterday morning, involving firefighters, police and Health Ministry staff.

items.title: Nepali duo break own records on Everest
items.summary: KATHMANDU: A Nepali climber dubbed the "Everest Man", Kami Rita Sherpa, scaled Mount Everest for a record 32nd time Sunday, while Lhakpa Sherpa broke her own women's record with an 11th summit. Kami Rita Sherpa, 56, first stood on the top of Mount Everest in 1994 while working for a commercial expedition. Lhakpa Sherpa, 52, known as the "Mountain Queen", first stood on the top of Everest in 2000, becoming the first Nepali woman to successfully summit and descend the world's highest peak.

items.title: Stay ready during Southwest Monsoon, CDF chief tells personnel
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia Civil Defence Force (CDF) personnel have been urged to remain prepared for any eventualities during the current Southwest Monsoon phase. CDF chief commissioner Mohammad Abiddin Abd Rahman said they must remain adaptive and proactive by leveraging their training and experience to ensure a swift response when needed. He said this while speaking at the Federal Territories (FT) CDF assembly here today, which was also attended by FT Civil Defence director Mohd Rosman Abdullah.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow urges Pakatan not to be complacent in Penang stronghold
items.summary: The Penang Chief Minister said the country’s political landscape has changed drastically, with voter sentiment shifting, as seen in the recent election results in Sabah. “Today, people want to see not just slogans, but leadership that can bring stability, development and confidence in the future of the state,” he said when delivering his speech at the PH 2026 Convention at the Persada International Convention Centre here today. In line with this shift in political sentiment, Chow said he has chosen to practise responsible politics rather than popularity politics, despite being in his final term as Chief Minister.

items.title: Negri Umno, BN yet to discuss state polls preparations
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Umno-Barisan Nasional (BN) has not held any discussions regarding preparations or actions for the upcoming state election. Mustapha was asked to comment on whether Negri Sembilan Umno-BN intends to contest all 36 state seats in the upcoming election, following Johor BN's recent move. In the 2023 Negri Sembilan state election, Umno-BN collaborated with Pakatan Harapan (PH) and won 14 state seats.

items.title: Century-old apam business earns Michelin Bib Gourmand honour
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: For nearly four hours each day, 65-year-old R. Neelamegan stands over open flames, enduring intense heat without complaint, his determination unshaken as he carries on his family's apam manis business, a legacy that has endured for more than a century. Using charcoal as fuel and clay pots imported from India, the traditional cooking method gives the apam a distinctive aroma that has become its signature, making it a daily breakfast favourite passed down through generations. Neelamegan, better known as Ravi among customers, said his father N. Rajagopal, who came from Tamil Nadu, India, started the apam manis business in 1922, alongside the opening of the coffee shop, which then charged a rent of just half a cent.

items.title: Senior police officer arrested over late-night extortion at Bukit Bintang restaurant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — An Assistant Superintendent of Police (ASP) was arrested last Thursday for allegedly being involved in an extortion case involving RM5,000 in cash in an incident at a restaurant in Bukit Bintang here on March 14. Dang Wangi police chief ACP Sazalee Adam said investigations were launched after a local man lodged a police report, expressing dissatisfaction with the suspect’s actions, who was believed to have handcuffed his colleague and threatened him to demand money. Sazalee said the suspect has been remanded for four days from yesterday until Tuesday to assist in investigations.

items.title: Loke: Negri Sembilan can dissolve assembly 'tomorrow'
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: DAP Negri Sembilan is ready to mirror Johor's political timeline, said DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today. The Seremban member of parliament said Negri Sembilan could dissolve its assembly "tomorrow" if Johor takes the lead. "If Johor contests all 56 seats, Negri Sembilan will contest 36.

items.title: Khaled says government to assess new opposition leader Samsuri
items.summary: MUAR: The government will monitor the performance of Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in his new role as opposition leader, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said the government would observe and assess performance in all positions, including the opposition leader. Samsuri, who is Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman and Terengganu menteri besar, will replace Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as opposition leader.

items.title: [UPDATED] Rafizi, Nik Nazmi take over Parti Bersama Malaysia
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former natural resources and environmental sustainability minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today took over Parti Bersama Malaysia. This was announced at an event here today, where the party's founder and president, Tan Gin Theam, presented a mock party registration certificate to Rafizi and Nik Nazmi to hand over the leadership of the party and officiate the transition. The party, also known as the Malaysian United Party, was established in 2016.

items.title: Pua questions PH-BN pact after Johor seat move
items.summary: The former Damansara member of parliament said Umno's decision to contest all 56 Johor state seats in the upcoming state election had exposed the party's "true colours". "By declaring their intent to contest all 56 state seats in Johor in the upcoming state elections, Umno has revealed its true colours, wanting its cake and eating it too. He said PH had done everything possible to maintain political stability under the Unity government, despite what he described as an "awkward arrangement" with BN following the 2022 general election.

items.title: Rafizi Ramli, Nik Nazmi take over Malaysian United Party
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 17 — A year after their departure from the Cabinet, PKR’s Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today announced that they will take over the Malaysian United Party (MUP) as their new political platform. MUP or Parti Bersama Malaysia is a little-known, Penang-based political party that was officially registered on December 2, 2016. Rafizi also said that the group had anticipated difficulties in registering a completely new political party.

items.title: Nearly 60pct of Malaysian workers overweight or obese
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Nearly 60 per cent of Malaysian workers who underwent health screenings were found to be overweight or obese, revealing a worrying health trend in the workforce. Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said the 59.2 per cent figure was significantly high and could not be taken lightly. Also present were Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (policy and international) and acting secretary-general Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar, Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail, Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (operations) Sutekno Ahmad Belon, Perkeso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal and Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.

items.title: KLIA police nab Chinese national who barged past autogates after burning through travel funds
items.summary: SEPANG, May 17 — A woman from China was detained after behaving aggressively while attempting to pass through the international departure gate at Terminal 1 of Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) yesterday. KLIA Police Chief ACP M. Ravi said in the 1.35 pm incident at International Departure Gate C, the suspect was found passing through the security inspection autogate without a valid ticket and acted aggressively when approached by security personnel for inspection. “Acting on information received, Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) KLIA auxiliary police detained the woman at 1.45 pm on the same day,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Civil Defence Force chief calls on personnel to remain adaptive, proactive during South-west Monsoon
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 17 — Malaysia Civil Defence Force (APM) personnel have been urged to remain prepared for any eventualities during the current South-west Monsoon phase. APM chief commissioner Mohammad Abiddin Abd Rahman said they must remain adaptive and proactive by leveraging their training and experience to ensure a swift response when needed. He said this while speaking at the Federal Territories (FT) APM assembly here today, which was also attended by FT Civil Defence director Mohd Rosman Abdullah.

items.title: Couple claims trial to charge of abusing 10‑year‑old nephew in Kota Bharu
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 17 — A married couple pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing their nephew last April. Wan Saiful Azuan Wan Idris, 31, and Yujas Nor Aida Jasmin Mohd Yusoff, 29, entered the plea after the charge was read out to them before Judge Zulkifli Abllah. — Bernama * If you suspect child abuse, call the following hotlines for free and confidential support: Talian Kasih at 15999 or WhatsApp 019-2615999 (24/7); Talian BuddyBear at 1800-18-2327(BEAR) (noon-midnight daily); and One Crisis Centre (24/7) Wilayah Persekutuan at 03-26155555 (Kuala Lumpur General Hospital), 03-61454333 (Sungai Buloh Hospital) or 03-83124200 (Putrajaya Hospital).

items.title: Anwar congratulates Perlis ruler Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin on 83rd birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has conveyed his congratulations to the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, on the occasion of His Royal Highness’s 83rd birthday today. In a Facebook post, Anwar prayed that Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin, Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Almarhum Tengku Abdul Rashid, and the Perlis royal family would continue to be blessed with Allah SWT’s mercy and guidance, and enjoy good health and well-being. “Daulat Tuanku,” he said.

items.title: Pakatan to field candidates in all 56 state seats in upcoming Johor polls after BN move, says Aminolhuda
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) today announced that it will contest in all 56 state constituencies in the upcoming polls, following Barisan Nasional’s (BN) announcement yesterday. Johor PH chief Aminolhuda Hassan said the decision was made after BN Johor announced that they would contest in all state seats on their own. He said Johor PH will not back down on this decision and dismissed any cooperation with BN for the state polls.

items.title: Minister: Defence Literacy Programme launched under Jiwa Murni Programme to boost public awareness on geopolitics
items.summary: MUAR, May 17 — The Defence Literacy Programme will be introduced as a new component under the Ministry of Defence’s Jiwa Murni Programme (PJM) this year, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. He said the programme, run by the Malaysian Institute of Defence and Security (MiDAS), aims to educate the public on geopolitics as part of the Comprehensive Defence (Hanruh) strategy. During the event, Mohamed Khaled presented a cheque for RM6 million for the 2025 Kementah Madani PJM implementation.

items.title: Nine MA63 issues resolved in three years under Madani govt, strongest record yet, says Mustapha Sakmud
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The Madani Government has achieved its best record in the nation’s history by resolving nine matters under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) within just three years of administration. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said the achievement surpassed earlier administrations, which managed to resolve only four matters over a longer period. “We have resolved nine matters within three years together with both state governments, with the participation of the Premier of Sarawak and the Chief Minister of Sabah.

items.title: Penang Pakatan-BN ties ‘strong and stable’, says Chow Kon Yeow amid Johor rift
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today affirmed that the political alliance between Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) in his state remains “strong and stable,” drawing a sharp contrast with the political developments in Johor. Speaking at the 2026 PH Convention here today, Chow insisted that Penang has prioritised political stability to safeguard the state’s future for its people, investors, and youth. “The people at present want stability, investors want stability, and young people looking for jobs and a future also want stability.” Chow, who also serves as the Penang PH chairman, explained his governance style, saying that he has opted to make responsible, albeit sometimes unpopular, decisions over populist moves to protect the state’s long-term interests.

items.title: Socso health screening shows 59.2pc of Malaysian workers overweight or obese, 19pc have diabetes, says deputy minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 17 — A total of 59.2 per cent of workers who underwent the Health Screening Programme organised by the Social Security Organisation (Socso) between 2023 and 2025 were found to be overweight or obese, said Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan. He said the findings also showed that about 19 per cent of them had diabetes, while 17.45 per cent suffered from high blood pressure. Also present Human Resources Ministry (KESUMA) deputy secretary-general (Policy and International), who is carrying out the duties of KESUMA secretary-general, Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar; Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail; Socso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal; and Socso Group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.

items.title: Pakatan convention to chart new strategies for nation’s future, says Fahmi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) 2026 Convention held today serves as the best platform for component parties of the coalition to formulate new strategies and approaches to ensure a brighter future for the country, said Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The PH 2026 Convention director said the convention, which is being held again after four years, provides delegates with the opportunity to pool ideas and energy for the benefit of Malaysians. He said this in his welcoming speech at the PH 2026 Convention at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here.

items.title: Kelantan peatlands under threat from open burning, land clearing; Fire Dept warns of flash flood risk
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 17 — Peatlands in Kelantan, which serve as natural water catchment areas, are increasingly threatened by open burning and land clearing for agricultural activities, raising concerns over a higher risk of flash floods in several parts of the state. State Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Farhan Sufyan Borhan said peatlands play a crucial role in storing water naturally due to their ability to absorb large volumes of water during heavy rainfall. He said most peatland areas in Kelantan are located in Bachok, Pasir Puteh and Pasir Mas, which are also identified as high-risk areas for open burning during the dry season.

items.title: Sarawak tourism minister hails international deep sea fishing tournament as major tourism event for Miri
items.summary: MIRI, May 17 — Events such as the Miri City International Deep Sea Fishing Tournament not only promote sports fishing and tourism, but also strengthen friendship, cultural exchange and international cooperation among participants from different countries. In this regard, Minister of Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts Sarawak Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah hailed the tournament as an important tourism event for Miri, and Sarawak in general. “This clearly shows that international interest in Miri’s fishing tourism remains strong and continues to grow.
